feedback.text: Video games are supposed to be an escape from reality for people, not a full blown storefront with a game as a secondary. In the current sphere companies hire psychologist to make store front as predatory as possible, while surfing on a thin line of morality, they pressure gamers of all ages to buy things to look cooler, and in many situations make people who dont have a bunch of cosmetic items, or boosters progressing slower than the rest of the community, we have gotten so much further away from gaming as an escape to it being a prison holding people addicted and in an eternal prison. Imagine trying out for your local football team and everyone on your team and people you will play against having spent thousands of euros to have speed boosts, stat boosts, dribbling bonuses, Etc. while you just bought some nice shoes, the required team outfit and shin guards. You are just there to have fun enjoying a sport you like. Its not a perfect analogy, but it fits into a lot of current games like call of duty, fifa and other games these days. Its not healthy for the gaming sphere as a whole, that the first thing that pops up when you start that new game, is deals on deals on deals, where 99% of items are boosts that make you better and cooler than everyone else. in 2023 the estimated value of every skin in Fortine was around 19300 dollars, imagine if this value was the actual price for the game? it would not fly, so instead they make the game "Free" and predatorily sell additional items of outrageous value with the quote "well you do not have to buy these cosmetic items", which does not fly. Kids, teens and adults alike all feel pressured into buying these things because everyone else has these things. Ive always been a fighter against these type of systems in games, because they add nothing to the game other than make players addicted and feel forced to interact with these systems. Gaming SHOULD be an escape and should not be a place where gamers get addicted and feel forced to buy things. Battlepasses, lootboxes and other in game microtransactions should all be heavily fought against, and its now or never we as a crowd need to fight against this type of thing. A lot of other hobbies already feel very protected, but gaming has been left in a state that actually ruins lives. Just look at how much money people have thrown into games like CS2(Previously CS:GO), values of items in this game have soared into the hundreds of thousands of dollars, sometimes even millions of dollars, tell me that kids, teens and adults dont feel tempted to gamble away life savings and the money they make on a monthly basis hoping to hit big and maybe save themselves from the losses they have accumilated. We need to tear these systems apart and bring back the casual games where people login every day and enjoy games, not logging in to fill a battlepass that will run out, catch that one skin that might never come back, or open a few lootboxes that might never show up again, because all of these systems cause chaos, and bring forth addiction that can and will ruin peoples lives.
